vibration_thresholds.source: ISO 10816-1 Class I (standard values — not found in PDF corpus)

failure_stages.name: Advanced damage
failure_stages.description: When flaking has proceeded to a certain stage, it makes its presence known in the form of noise and vibra- tions, which serve as a warning that it is time to change the bearing. The causes of premature flaking may be heavier external loading than had been anticipated, preloading on account of incorr
failure_stages.vibration_indicator: high vibration, audible noise, elevated temperature

failure_stages.name: Subsurface initiation
failure_stages.description: Bearing life and bearing failures Most bearings outlive the machinery in which they are installed . Only some fail .

failure_stages.name: Microscopic spalling
failure_stages.description: Inspection and troubleshooting When a problem occurs, inspection during operation or standstill and immediate trouble shooting can give hints about what is happening . The very important subject of condition moni- toring (finding damage in time) is not covered here . More information about c

failure_stages.name: Visible spalling
failure_stages.description: Path patterns Once damage has occurred, the bearing needs to be examined and analyzed . Analysis requires a good understanding of path patterns .

failure_stages.name: Advanced damage
failure_stages.description: ISO failure modes classification Terminology and the ISO classification system helps to communicate the type of damage and its possible causes .

failure_stages.name: Severe damage
failure_stages.description: Damage and actions A number of cases are described together with corrective actions . In this chapter, only non- destructive analysis is covered .

failure_stages.name: Catastrophic failure
failure_stages.description: Other investigations For complex cases, there are several advanced and destructive methods for failure analysis available at SKF’s labo- ratories . This chapter provides a brief summary .

failure_stages.name: Stage 7
failure_stages.description: Case studies Bearing damage analysis can be quite complex . This is demonstrated with a few case studies .

failure_stages.name: Stage 8
failure_stages.description: Appendices Appendices A to E contain key charts for quick overviews, hints about how to collect bearing damage information, and a glossary for easy reference . failure_stages.name: Microscopic spalling
failure_stages.description: First spall, detected by SKF enveloped acceleration technology .

failure_stages.name: Visible spalling
failure_stages.description: Spalling has developed to an extent that the damage can be detected by standard vibration monitoring .

failure_stages.name: Advanced damage
failure_stages.description: Advanced spalling causes high vibration and noise levels and an increase in operating temperature .
failure_stages.vibration_indicator: high vibration, audible noise, elevated temperature

failure_stages.name: Severe damage
failure_stages.description: Severe damage occurs: fatigue fracture of the bearing inner ring .

failure_stages.name: Catastrophic failure
failure_stages.description: Catastrophic failure occurs with sec- ondary damage to other components . Monitoring noise and vibration A common method used to try to identify deterioration or damage in a bearing is by listening . Bearings that are in good condi- tion produce a soft purring noise .
